description: Benchmark source pairs for generating and reviewing source2launch promotion packs against real open-source launch material.

items.name: Source2Launch
items.whyUseful: Uses the current project as a self-benchmark before testing external projects.
reviewFocus: Does the copy explain that source2launch generates platform-ready promotion content from repos, papers, PDFs, and text?
Does it avoid reverting to old star-growth diagnosis language?
Does it mention review-before-publish and browser-assisted draft filling accurately?
Does it propose concrete visuals such as README crops, paper clips, or social cards?

items.name: Segment Anything
items.whyUseful: A strong reference for turning a research release into a public launch: clear problem, model, dataset, demo, paper, and code.
reviewFocus: Does the generated hook state the image segmentation problem in concrete terms?
Does it connect code, paper, dataset, and demo without overclaiming?
Does it recommend visual proof from masks, architecture, dataset scale, or demo screenshots?
Does it adapt the same source into X/Twitter, Zhihu, Xiaohongshu, WeChat, Product Hunt, and Show HN structures?
commands: source2launch promote https://github.com/facebookresearch/segment-anything --platform launch --prompt-preset paper,visual --prompt-preset launchkit --json --output examples/generated/segment-anything.json

items.name: Whisper
items.whyUseful: A concise launch reference where a model, paper, open-source code, model card, and clear developer use cases are tied together.
reviewFocus: Does the copy explain speech recognition and translation without becoming too generic?
Does it ground claims in multilingual data, robustness, open-source code, or the model card?
Does it suggest practical clips such as model architecture, training diagram, or command usage?
Does it keep caveats about benchmark specialization and zero-shot use visible?
commands: source2launch promote https://github.com/openai/whisper --platform all --prompt-preset paper,technical,visual --json --output examples/generated/whisper.json

items.name: vLLM
items.whyUseful: A technical infrastructure launch with a sharp systems claim, named mechanism, performance angle, and installable repo.
reviewFocus: Does the hook name the serving bottleneck or PagedAttention instead of saying only 'faster inference'?
Does the copy make the try path concrete for developers?
Does it separate paper evidence from blog explanation?
Does it avoid unsupported throughput numbers unless the source extraction found them?
commands: source2launch promote https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm --platform launch --prompt-preset launchkit,technical --json --output examples/generated/vllm.json

items.name: AutoGen
items.whyUseful: A good agent-framework example with paper framing, docs, blog positioning, human-in-the-loop language, and platform-specific launch potential.
reviewFocus: Does the generated content explain multi-agent conversation with concrete workflow examples?
Does it mention human feedback and tool use without implying unsafe autonomy?
Does it account for the repository's current maintenance-mode status if the source extractor sees it?
Does the long-form version read like a credible technical explanation rather than a broad AI-agent advertisement?
commands: source2launch promote https://github.com/microsoft/autogen --platform all --prompt-preset paper,launchkit,technical --json --output examples/generated/autogen.json

items.name: LLaVA
items.whyUseful: A multimodal research project where paper, demo, data, model, and visual examples can be turned into social posts and carousel cards.
reviewFocus: Does the copy explain visual instruction tuning in plain language?
Does it call out demo and visual examples early?
Does the Xiaohongshu version become a carousel plan rather than a generic paragraph?
Does it avoid making capability claims beyond what the project page or paper supports?
commands: source2launch promote https://github.com/haotian-liu/LLaVA --platform all --prompt-preset paper,visual,zhihu,xhs --json --output examples/generated/llava.json

items.name: Dify
items.whyUseful: A product-led open-source benchmark where the reference material is closer to SaaS positioning than a research paper.
reviewFocus: Does the generated launch copy explain who builds with it and what they can ship?
Does it show a workflow/RAG/agent/product angle without stuffing every feature into the hook?
Does it suggest product screenshots or workflow diagrams for image generation?
Does the Show HN version stay plain and tryable?
commands: source2launch promote https://github.com/langgenius/dify --platform launch --prompt-preset launchkit,technical,visual --json --output examples/generated/dify.json
